metadata.title: Plan Before Acting
metadata.description_blurb: Agents plan whether you ask them to or not. Requiring a visible plan before execution surfaces the interpretation that was already there — at zero cost.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Agents plan whether you ask them to or not. The ReAct architecture always sequences — it decides what to do before it does it. The question is whether that plan is readable before execution, or whether it runs invisible and you find out at the end. Requiring a plan costs nothing and surfaces the interpretation that was already there. This is Liam, in for Bear.

props.command: Before acting, show me your plan: goal, inputs, steps, permissions needed, stop conditions, and what evidence will show the task is complete.

beats.narration_text: The invisible plan and the visible plan contain the same information. The difference is that you cannot correct a plan you have not read. Visibility is not a courtesy — it is the control system.
beats.new_visual_element: Invisible vs visible plan diagram — same data, different control

beats.narration_text: A real plan has eight elements: goal, inputs, tools, sequence, permissions needed, reversibility of each step, stop conditions, and the evidence that will show the task is complete. A plan that skips any of these is not a plan — it is a caption.

Here is the difference. Caption plan: 'I will review the files and prepare a summary.' Sounds competent. Approves nothing. Real plan: three specific files, read-only access, no browser, output to reports slash draft dot md, stop if any file is outside the named folder, evidence is a row count matching the input. One of these you can correct. One you cannot.
beats.new_visual_element: Side-by-side: caption plan vs real plan

beats.narration_text: The second rule: if the plan changes mid-task in a way that affects the tools used, the data touched, or the risk level — the agent stops and reports before proceeding. Report after is an audit log. Ask before is supervision. Only before catches the error.
beats.new_visual_element: Material plan-change rule — stop before scope expands

beats.narration_text: Two habits. Before execution: require the plan, read it, correct it. Mid-execution: material scope change means stop and ask. Together they give you the control that the agentic loop otherwise skips entirely.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — plan-first gate + material-change rule on one page

beats.narration_text: Use this. Paste it into your next agentic task — before any execution starts.

props.command: Before acting on anything, show me your plan: goal, inputs, tools you will use, sequence of steps, permissions needed, reversibility of each step, stop conditions, and the evidence that will show the task is complete. Wait for my approval before executing.

beats.narration_text: If this gives you your first real plan review, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
